methodology: Combined executable-line and branch-outcome coverage from Cobertura XML

features.name: Repository analysis and graph construction
features.why_core: Creates the evidence graph that every Explore, Ask, Onboarding, Issues, and MCP workflow consumes.

features.name: Revision-aware indexing and graph retrieval
features.why_core: Keeps repository evidence current and supplies bounded lexical, vector, symbol, and graph context to agents.

features.name: Grounded agent answering, validation, and memory
features.why_core: Implements multi-round repository investigation, structured answers, citation validation, comparison, and conversation memory.

features.name: Adaptive onboarding routes and missions
features.why_core: Turns repository evidence into role-, objective-, experience-, and time-specific learning routes and contribution missions.

features.name: Secure repository import
features.why_core: Places a local path or public GitHub repository inside the analyzed workspace while enforcing repository security limits.

supporting_subsystems.name: Provider and SDK adapters
supporting_subsystems.reason: Vendor transport integration; exercised by adapter unit tests and live evaluations but excluded from the core business-logic denominator.

supporting_subsystems.name: HTTP and MCP delivery adapters
supporting_subsystems.reason: Interface wiring around core services; covered separately by integration and E2E tests.

supporting_subsystems.name: Subprocess execution infrastructure
supporting_subsystems.reason: Generic bounded process runner used by secure import, not the repository-import policy itself.

supporting_subsystems.name: Issues secondary feature
supporting_subsystems.reason: Week 7 secondary feature rather than the primary onboarding/grounded-RAG business path; still included in whole-backend coverage.

supporting_subsystems.name: Observability infrastructure
supporting_subsystems.reason: Cross-cutting trace instrumentation; verified separately through hook tests and live JSONL evidence.
